sanskrit_devanagari: भूतग्राम: स एवायं भूत्वा भूत्वा प्रलीयते ।रात्र्यागमेऽवश: पार्थ प्रभवत्यहरागमे ॥ १९ ॥
verse_text_iast: bhūta-grāmaḥ sa evāyaṁbhūtvā bhūtvā pralīyaterātry-āgame ’vaśaḥ pārthaprabhavaty ahar-āgame
synonyms: bhūta-grāmaḥ — the aggregate of all living entities; saḥ — these; eva — certainly; ayam — this; bhūtvā bhūtvā — repeatedly taking birth; pralīyate — is annihilated; rātri — of night; āgame — on the arrival; avaśaḥ — automatically; pārtha — O son of Pṛthā; prabhavati — is manifest; ahaḥ — of daytime; āgame — on the arrival.
translation: Again and again, when Brahmā’s day arrives, all living entities come into being, and with the arrival of Brahmā’s night they are helplessly annihilated.
purport_excerpt: The less intelligent, who try to remain within this material world, may be elevated to higher planets and then again must come down to this planet earth.
